body.title: Track build-time per ticket; calibrate orphan watchdog timeout
body.result: dispatched_at field + wall_minutes in corpus + p90_minutes method + watchdog auto-calibration via 3×p90 when n≥10
body.description:
The orphan watchdog (T-granny-orphan-watchdog) uses fixed size-keyed timeouts. Those defaults are guesses. We have 1007 completed tickets with claimed_at/completed_at — the data to calibrate from exists. But claimed_at measures queue wait, not build time. We need dispatched_at (when Granny handed it off) and started_at (when the CC session actually opened the ticket).

Proposed shape:
1. Add `dispatched_at` timestamp to the ticket record in cc_queue.py when Granny calls cc_queue.py setstatus in_progress (i.e. at dispatch time). This separates 'queued' from 'Granny acted on it'.
2. PatternTracker.record_outcome() already takes cost_usd/iterations — add wall_minutes=(completed_at - dispatched_at) to the outcome record in the JSONL corpus.
3. Add PatternTracker.p90_minutes(size) method: reads corpus, filters by size + DONE signal, returns p90 of wall_minutes. Returns None when n<10.
4. GrannyDaemon.run_once() calls p90_minutes(size) for the watchdog timeout fallback: `timeout = p90_minutes(size) * 3 if p90_minutes(size) else DEFAULT_TIMEOUT[size]`.

**Affected files:** lab/claudecode/cc_queue.py (dispatched_at field), devices/granny/daemon.py (PatternTracker.p90_minutes + watchdog calibration), devices/granny/escalation_corpus.py (wall_minutes field in append_outcome)
**Design rules:** flat-file only for corpus; no new Postgres tables
**Scope boundary:** dispatched_at + wall_minutes recording + p90 lookup; not a full analytics dashboard; started_at (requires CC-side change) deferred
**Completion criteria:** after 10+ DONE outcomes recorded with wall_minutes, Granny's watchdog timeout for that size class switches from the fixed default to 3×p90_minutes; log line `GRANNY_TIMEOUT_CALIBRATED|size=S|p90=<n>m|n=<count>` visible on next Granny restart
